root_cause_candidates.cause: Significant downstream latency spike in ts-order-service (p95 Δms > 100 ms; cpu_z +4.7) likely causing cascading increased latency observed in ts-preserve-service, which depends on ts-order-service. No error logs detected; root cause inferred from metric anomaly and dependency propagation.
root_cause_candidates.reasoning: All three sources align that ts-preserve-service’s degraded performance is tied to its dependency on ts-order-service. The Log Agent identifies ts-order-service as the primary tail-latency producer and notes downstream impact. The Topology Agent confirms a direct ts-preserve-service → ts-order-service path with plausible propagation. The TCB-RCA algorithm, while ranking ts-preserve-service as the root, contradicts the log/topology insights; given the observed upstream latency without local error signals in ts-order-service, the deeper, upstream root is more credible to be ts-order-service. Therefore, ts-order-service is the most plausible root cause.

final_verdict.cause: Significant downstream latency spike in ts-order-service (p95 Δms > 100 ms; cpu_z +4.7) likely causing cascading increased latency observed in ts-preserve-service, which depends on ts-order-service. No error logs detected; root cause inferred from metric anomaly and dependency propagation.

metadata.llm_reasoning: Metric signals show ts-order-service has the strongest tail-latency anomaly (p95_Δms +244.2 ms, cpu_z +4.7), indicating a root cause. ts-preserve-service and ts-ui-dashboard show elevated metrics but not as pronounced. Given ts-preserve-service depends on ts-order-service, the upstream latency likely cascaded downstream. No error logs are present, so signals rely on metric anomalities and the known dependency path.
